response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, hands-on engineer with a current Staff title at Google and directly relevant AI-native experience building MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling, plus strong TypeScript and real high-throughput systems work at Sociomantic. However, Python — a must-have — is entirely absent from the resume, the deep distributed-systems work is several years old and concentrated in the niche D language, and the resume reads as strong senior IC delivery with little evidence of directing technical strategy or managing cross-functional stakeholders at staff scope.
strengths: Current Google Staff engineer contributing to AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic-workflow frameworks
Strong TypeScript/Node.js across multiple senior roles (Google, Meta, RIDE Capital)
Real high-throughput experience: low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second
concerns: No Python listed anywhere despite it being a must-have skill
Little demonstrated staff-level technical strategy, stakeholder coordination, or decision-driving at scale
Distributed-systems depth is dated (2018-2020) and tied to the niche DLang ecosystem

key_factors.factor: AI-native / MCP and agentic tooling experience
